title: How Gilgit-Baltistan's Sectarian Divide Was Built, Not Born
summary_full: Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) was a syncretic region where Shias, Sunnis, and Ismailis coexisted, traded, and intermarried. However, over the last few decades, this social cohesion has been systematically eroded by political elites.
